title: SSRF, DNS Rebinding, Injection, Response Validation, and Least Authority · Browser and Node Clie… · Inference Brief: Response Content Trust
summary: A server-rendering image proxy and browser preview must address SSRF, DNS rebinding, CORS limits, hostile media types, and privacy-safe diagnostics. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http ts response content trust increment in a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch defense-in-depth outbound request policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Verify media type, size, schema, provenance, redirects, signatures when required, and sink-specific encoding before use. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Reject control characters and unsafe dynamic structure in targets and fields and rely on maintained encoders. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
